title: Recover A Fully Discharged AGV By Manual Charging And Returning It To The System
summary: Recover an AGV that is fully discharged and cannot move by removing it from RMS, removing its tote, disabling a charger for exclusive use, manually charging the AGV until it boots, then adding it back into the system so it can charge itself or resume normal operation.
when_to_use: Use when an AGV is fully discharged or 'dead' and cannot move normally, and the source-described recovery method is to manually charge it until it boots and can be returned to the system.
not_for: Do not use when site-specific recovery controls are required but are not provided in the source.
Do not use when the AGV cannot be safely moved.
Do not use when the operator needs exact RMS remove or re-add UI steps that are not provided in the source.

responsibilities: Identify the fully discharged AGV recovery case.
Remove the tote from the AGV.
Disable and later restore charger availability as needed for manual charging.
Manually position the AGV for charging.
Add the AGV back into the system after it boots.

safety_notes: This procedure is not support-safe because it includes manual handling of a non-moving AGV.
Stop if the AGV cannot be safely moved.
The source does not provide detailed physical safety controls, floor access controls, or LOTO requirements.
access_or_tools_needed: Access to charger disable and enable controls
Access to a charger or manual charger
Ability to remove the tote from the AGV

system_preconditions: The AGV is fully discharged and cannot move normally.
A charger can be reserved for manual charging by disabling it for normal allocation.

visual_references.description: Charger Selection view showing charger status and the disable function used to reserve a charger for manual charging recovery.

steps.title: Identify the fully discharged AGV
steps.purpose: Confirm that the recovery applies to an AGV that is fully discharged and cannot move normally.
steps.instruction: Identify the AGV that is fully discharged or 'dead' and will not move normally, matching the manual charging recovery case described in the source.
steps.expected_result: The affected AGV is identified as a manual charging recovery candidate.
steps.healthy_condition: The AGV is recognized as the non-moving, fully discharged unit intended for manual charging recovery.
steps.failure_condition: The AGV condition is unclear, or the situation does not match the source-described fully discharged recovery case.

actions.label: Confirm dead AGV condition
actions.exact_action: Identify the AGV that is fully discharged and will not move normally.

stop_or_escalate_if: The AGV condition does not clearly match the source-described fully discharged recovery case.
Site-specific recovery controls are required but not provided in the source.

source_evidence.quote_or_summary: There were some AGVs that were dead; you had to physically remove them, remove the AGV from RMS, remove the tote, disable the chargers, and manually charge them.

steps.title: Remove the AGV from RMS
steps.purpose: Take the fully discharged AGV out of system control before manual recovery actions.
steps.instruction: Remove the AGV from RMS as described in the source before proceeding with tote removal and manual charging recovery.
steps.expected_result: The AGV is removed from RMS.
steps.healthy_condition: The AGV is no longer active in RMS for normal system-directed operation.
steps.failure_condition: The AGV cannot be removed from RMS, or the required RMS details are not available in the source.
stop_or_escalate_if: Removing the AGV in RMS requires details not present in the source.

source_evidence.quote_or_summary: There were some AGVs that were dead; you had to physically remove them, remove the AGV from RMS, remove the tote, disable the chargers, and manually charge them.

steps.title: Remove the tote from the AGV
steps.purpose: Prepare the AGV for recovery and align with the source-described sequence before manual charging.
steps.instruction: Remove the tote from the AGV.
steps.expected_result: The AGV is without a tote and ready for manual charging recovery.
steps.healthy_condition: The tote has been removed from the AGV.
steps.failure_condition: The tote cannot be removed or tote handling requires details not provided in the source.

actions.label: Remove tote
actions.exact_action: Remove the tote from the AGV.

stop_or_escalate_if: The tote cannot be safely removed.
Tote handling requires unsupported site-specific details.

source_evidence.quote_or_summary: There were some AGVs that were dead; you had to physically remove them, remove the AGV from RMS, remove the tote, disable the chargers, and manually charge them.

steps.title: Disable a charger for manual charging use
steps.purpose: Reserve a charger so RMS does not allocate it to another AGV during manual charging recovery.
steps.instruction: Disable the charger or chargers needed to support the manual charging activity so the location is not used for normal allocation.
steps.expected_result: The selected charger is logically unavailable for normal AGV allocation and can be used for the recovery.
steps.healthy_condition: The charger shows disabled status for allocation purposes, and RMS will not route other AGVs there.
steps.failure_condition: The charger cannot be disabled, or charger status/control details are insufficient for the recovery.

actions.label: Disable charger
actions.exact_action: Disable the charger or chargers needed to support the manual charging activity so the location is not used for normal allocation.

screens_or_images.what_to_look_at: Charger Selection view, charger ID, charger enabled/disabled status, and the disable function used to reserve a charger for manual charging.

stop_or_escalate_if: The charger disable function is not available.
The operator cannot confirm the charger is reserved from normal allocation.

source_evidence.quote_or_summary: There were some AGVs that were dead; you had to physically remove them, remove the AGV from RMS, remove the tote, disable the chargers, and manually charge them.
A charger can be logically disabled so RMS and WCS do not send other AGVs to that charging location, allowing a low-battery or dead AGV to be manually moved onto the charger for a charging boost.
Disable does not kill the charger itself; it logically disables the charger so RMS will not route AGVs to that charging location.

steps.title: Manually position the AGV for charging
steps.purpose: Place the fully discharged AGV onto the reserved charger or use the site manual charger method described by the source.
steps.instruction: Manually move the AGV onto a charger, or use the manual charger mentioned in the source if that is the site method.
steps.expected_result: The AGV is physically positioned for manual charging.
steps.healthy_condition: The AGV is on the intended charger or connected to the manual charging method described by the source.
steps.failure_condition: The AGV cannot be safely moved or cannot be positioned for charging.

actions.label: Move AGV to charger
actions.exact_action: Manually move the AGV onto a charger, or use the manual charger mentioned in the source if that is the site method.

screens_or_images.what_to_look_at: Charger Selection context showing the charger reserved for manual charging recovery.

stop_or_escalate_if: The AGV cannot be safely moved.
Site-specific manual charging controls are required but not provided in the source.

source_evidence.quote_or_summary: There were some AGVs that were dead; you had to physically remove them, remove the AGV from RMS, remove the tote, disable the chargers, and manually charge them.
A disabled charger can be used so the desired AGV can be pushed into that charging station.

steps.title: Wait for the AGV to boot
steps.purpose: Allow the AGV to gain enough charge to power on and become eligible for return to the system.
steps.instruction: Wait until the AGV has enough charge to boot up.
steps.expected_result: The AGV boots after receiving enough charge.
steps.healthy_condition: The AGV powers on after manual charging.
steps.failure_condition: The AGV does not boot after manual charging.

actions.label: Monitor for boot
actions.exact_action: Wait until the AGV has enough charge to boot up.

stop_or_escalate_if: The AGV does not boot after manual charging.

source_evidence.quote_or_summary: After a while, once it gets enough to use, the robot boots up and then you can add it back in the system and it'll go charge itself.

steps.title: Add the AGV back into the system
steps.purpose: Return the recovered AGV to system control after it has booted.
steps.instruction: Add the AGV back into the system after it boots.
steps.expected_result: The AGV is returned to the system.
steps.healthy_condition: The AGV is recognized in the system again after boot.
steps.failure_condition: The AGV cannot be re-added, or the source does not provide enough detail to complete the re-add action.

actions.label: Add AGV back
actions.exact_action: Add the AGV back into the system after it boots.

stop_or_escalate_if: Re-adding the AGV in RMS requires details not present in the source.

source_evidence.quote_or_summary: After a while, once it gets enough to use, the robot boots up and then you can add it back in the system and it'll go charge itself.

steps.title: Return the AGV to normal charging or operation
steps.purpose: Complete recovery by allowing the AGV to resume system-directed charging or normal activity.
steps.instruction: Allow the AGV to go charge itself or resume system-directed operation as described in the source.
steps.expected_result: The AGV resumes charging or normal system-controlled activity.
steps.healthy_condition: The AGV proceeds under system control to charge itself or continue normal operation.
steps.failure_condition: The AGV does not resume expected system-controlled behavior after being re-added.

actions.label: Verify resumed operation
actions.exact_action: Allow the AGV to go charge itself or resume system-directed operation as described in the source.

stop_or_escalate_if: The AGV does not resume charging or normal system-controlled activity.

source_evidence.quote_or_summary: After a while, once it gets enough to use, the robot boots up and then you can add it back in the system and it'll go charge itself.

success_criteria: The previously non-moving AGV gains enough charge to boot.
The AGV is added back into the system.
The AGV goes to charge itself or resumes normal system-controlled activity.
healthy_conditions: The AGV is correctly identified as a fully discharged recovery case.
The AGV is removed from RMS before manual recovery actions.
The tote is removed from the AGV.
A charger is logically disabled so RMS does not allocate it to another AGV.
The AGV is manually positioned for charging.
The AGV boots after receiving enough charge.
The AGV is successfully added back into the system.
The AGV resumes charging or normal operation under system control.
failure_conditions: The AGV does not boot after manual charging.
The AGV cannot be safely moved.
Removing or re-adding the AGV in RMS requires details not present in the source.
Site-specific recovery controls are required but not provided in the source.
The AGV does not resume expected system-controlled behavior after re-add.
escalation_guidance: Escalate if the AGV does not boot after manual charging.
Escalate if the AGV cannot be safely moved.
Escalate if site-specific recovery controls are required but not provided in the source.
Escalate if removing or re-adding the AGV in RMS requires details not present in the source.

screens_or_images.what_to_look_at: Charger Selection screen showing charger status, enabled/disabled state, and charger context for manual charging recovery.

source_refs.quote_or_summary: There were some AGVs that were dead; you had to physically remove them, remove the AGV from RMS, remove the tote, disable the chargers, and manually charge them.
After a while, once it gets enough to use, the robot boots up and then you can add it back in the system and it'll go charge itself.

missing_details: The source does not provide exact RMS UI steps for removing the AGV.
The source does not provide exact RMS UI steps for adding the AGV back into the system.
The source does not provide a detailed physical movement method for the AGV.
The source does not provide explicit charger re-enable as part of this candidate workflow, although related artifact text indicates chargers can be re-enabled after recovery.
The source does not provide a time estimate for charging or recovery completion.
The source does not provide explicit production stop or LOTO requirements.
